howItWorks.intro: This dovetail joint spacing calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Tail Width = (Board Width - 2 x Half-Pin) / Number of Tails
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What angle should dovetails be?
faq.answer: Use 1:6 (about 9.5 degrees) for softwood and 1:8 (about 7 degrees) for hardwood. The steeper softwood angle provides more mechanical lock in weaker wood. Many hand-tool woodworkers use 1:7 as a general-purpose angle for all species.

faq.question: How many tails should a dovetail joint have?
faq.answer: The number of tails is aesthetic preference, but common practice is one tail per inch of board width minus one. A 6-inch board often has 3-4 tails. Wider tails look bolder and traditional; narrower tails look refined. Function is the same either way.

faq.question: Is the Dovetail Joint Spacing Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What board width should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate board width value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Dovetail Joint Spacing Calculator?
sections.body: The Dovetail Joint Spacing Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your board width, number of tails, half-pin width, you get instant results including tail width, pin spacing, dovetail angle. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting tail width right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your board width and need to find the right tail width. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Board Width (inches)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your board width — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of tails, half-pin width, tail angle ratio. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your tail width, pin spacing, dovetail angle. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your board width before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both tail width and pin spacing — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your tail angle ratio, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
